entry.title: Homological Stability for Moduli Spaces of Manifolds
entry.summary: We prove homological stability results for classifying spaces of diffeomorphism groups of high-dimensional manifolds. The main theorem establishes stability in a range increasing with the genus, resolving a conjecture of Galatius and Randal-Williams. Our methods combine techniques from surgery theory, homotopy theory, and the theory of operads to construct explicit stabilization maps. We also obtain secondary stability results that describe the first unstable homology groups in terms of algebraic K-theory. These results have applications to the topology of cobordism categories and topological field theories.

entry.title: On the Homological Stability of Configuration Spaces
entry.summary: This paper studies the homological stability of unordered configuration spaces of open manifolds with respect to adding particles. We give a new proof of the classical result of McDuff and Segal using the framework of factorization homology. Our approach yields improved stability ranges and extends to configuration spaces with twisted coefficients. As an application, we compute the rational cohomology of configuration spaces of punctured surfaces in the stable range and relate it to the cohomology of certain graph complexes studied by Kontsevich. The methods are based on a careful analysis of the scanning map and its interaction with the Dold-Thom theorem. We also discuss connections to representation stability in the sense of Church, Ellenberg, and Farb, providing new evidence for their conjectures in the manifold setting.
